reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #memory-fog-opaque-not-translucent #grok-refute-and-fix: remembered-areas = an OPAQUE dim colour overlay over the dark base (source-over keeps mask alpha=1 -> NO live-board leak); a partial-ERASE approach (my first draft) WOULD have leaked (translucent fog reveals the live board) -- caught in self-review pre-commit. Council ADOPT HYBRID (pure unit-testable grid for explored state, proven RT for live fog). GROK-ANALYST refuted a phantom bilinear-leak (opaque base) and caught a real centre-only coverage gap (-> circle-rect overlap). Lesson: in screen-space fog, 'dim' must be an opaque colour change, never an alpha reduction, or it leaks the board.

SESSION #method-opaque-not-translucent-fog: in SCREEN-SPACE fog, a 'dim/remembered' tier MUST be an OPAQUE colour change over the dark base, never an alpha reduction -- alpha reduction makes the overlay translucent and leaks the live board (the rejected M1). Proof: source-over onto an opaque base keeps out.a = a_src + 1*(1-a_src) = 1 for any source alpha, so an opaque lighter colour gives dim-but-opaque. Caught my own partial-ERASE draft pre-commit by reasoning the blend math. Transferable: when a render tier must 'show less' without revealing what's beneath, change colour at full opacity; don't lower alpha.

SESSION #method-prime-audit-refute-not-rubberstamp: PRIME-AUDIT independently REFUTED Gemini's inverted-layer composite recipe AND Grok's phantom 'bilinear leak' (both dissolved on blend-math), while keeping terrain-dim against a false R2 consensus on principled grounds (literal ask + zero-ghost-risk) AND a REAL centre-only grid-coverage bug got caught+fixed. The discipline 'for each adopted/flagged item, state what would falsify it INDEPENDENTLY of the claimant' caught 2 false + held 1 contested + surfaced 1 true. Earns its keep on every render/GPU feature where reviewers reason about compositing.

SESSION #method-checkpoint-under-degraded-tooling: when the tool-output channel degrades mid-batch (delayed bursts, repeated empty probes), checkpoint at the nearest CLEAN priority boundary and carry forward the riskiest remaining work rather than grind it through broken tooling -- quality of the risky piece (P2 ghost state machine) > cramming the batch. Validated: P1 shipped clean + deployed; P2/P3 carried with full Council design intact.

P2 #memory-fog-cpu-gate-not-gpu-mask #pixel-e2e-caught-mask-attenuation: the S59-designed 'memoryLayer masked by Sprite(maskRT)' was empirically WRONG -- a Pixi v8 sprite mask attenuates the masked content by the mask's BRIGHTNESS, and the fog mask is near-black (MEMORY_FOG_COLOR 0x161b2e), so it crushed the ghost silhouettes to ~5% alpha (measured g=35 vs expected ~121 in the pixel e2e). Pivoted to a CPU per-sprite visibility gate (sprite.visible = !isPointVisible(sources, ghost.pos)), reusing the SAME vision predicate as the memory update -- simpler, no brightness coupling, and the GPU mask wasn't viable anyway (a fog shader is Council-vetoed in this codebase). Lesson: a render-compositing assumption from static design -- even a Council+PRIME-AUDIT-ratified 'sprite.mask=alpha' -- must be runtime-pixel-verified; 'sprite.mask=alpha' is FALSE for a dark mask (it's brightness-weighted). RALPH:PATROL separately caught a missing match-start ghost reset (match 2 would inherit match 1 ghosts). PRIME-AUDIT empirically REFUTED both GROK REALs (placedBy-immutable grep) + downgraded a GEMINI design-pref. Transferable: when a reviewer OR your own design asserts GPU-compositing behavior, the pixel e2e is the arbiter, not the reasoning.

P3 #fog-polish-reuse-existing-state-edge: the win-lift FREEZE is a 1-line guard (if target===0 return) placed AFTER the alpha/visibility assignment but BEFORE the throttled recompose -> fog+ghosts fade via alpha over a FROZEN mask composition (uniform dissolve), and it self-releases because target>0 on the next PLAYING edge (no stuck-frozen risk; the win test still completes to alpha 0). reset() reused the EXISTING main.ts:680 *->TITLE teardown edge (already abort-cutscene/reset-shake/clear-creatures) rather than instrumenting all 3 RETURN_TO_TITLE dispatch sites -> one idempotent call covers lobby-Back + POSTGAME->title + peer-drop. Lesson: when adding teardown/reset for a NEW subsystem, find the existing state-transition edge that already orchestrates sibling teardown and co-locate there (single idempotent site) instead of touching every dispatch caller. Each polish item earned a dedicated e2e proof (freeze: a vision hole doesn't relocate mid-lift; reset: rememberedCount->0) -- runtime-verifying GPU/state behavior, not just tsc.
